title: Kneiffia linifolia
taxonomy: Kneiffia linifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is classified as a type of orchid, belonging to the subfamily Orchidoideae. The plant is further divided into the genus Kneiffia, which contains a small number of species. The genus Kneiffia is a part of the tribe Cranichideae, which includes other orchid species. Kneiffia linifolia is native to Mexico and Central America, where it grows in the wild.

intro: Kneiffia linifolia, also known as Desert Rose or Lady's Finger Cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family Cactaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Kneiffia linifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kneiffia linifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as to promote wound healing.
